summary: Track remaining follow-up work after stabilizing auto-improve telemetry preservation, clarifying workspace copy-count semantics, and reserving workspace_preparation policy mode.

observations.problem: The sparse_manifest workspace mode previously had only a reserved policy/schema field and no deterministic runtime copy or manifest-diff universe.
observations.why_it_matters: A sparse workspace is only safe if omitted baseline files cannot appear as false deletions and if existing shadow/rehearsal/live apply guards remain the authority for writes.
observations.automation_candidate: Implemented a materialized copied-universe sparse workspace planner that derives copy entries from allowed_apply_roots, primary/supporting/test targets, and declared dependencies, then records the diff universe in changed-files manifest and run telemetry.
observations.suggested_followup: Sparse mode is now automated with copied-universe diff semantics. Future optimization should tune declared read dependencies for full-vault candidate capture cost, but should keep manifest diff scoped to the copied universe and apply guards bound to allowed_apply_roots.

observations.problem: Auto-improve iteration telemetry now preserves the current workspace/apply fields, but preservation is still maintained by an explicit allowlist rather than a schema-derived or contract-tested merge policy for future telemetry fields.
observations.why_it_matters: Future run-telemetry additions can be silently dropped by later iteration writes if the preservation allowlist is not updated alongside the schema and writer tests.
observations.automation_candidate: Implemented a named iteration telemetry contract that separates explicit overwrite fields, merged fields, and preserved carry-over fields, then added a schema parity regression so new run-telemetry properties must be classified instead of being silently dropped.
observations.suggested_followup: If run-telemetry grows more phase-local sections, keep extending the overwrite/merge contract intentionally; a fully schema-derived runtime merge can stay deferred until there is a concrete need beyond the current parity guardrail.
